chief_complaint: An 81-year-old man presented with progressive dyspnea.
history: The patient had a history of metabolic syndrome, stable cardiac disease, and was diagnosed with high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the renal pelvis with pulmonary metastases. Two months after nephroureterectomy and during first-line carboplatin chemotherapy.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: ECG showed ST-segment elevations suggestive of acute myocardial infarction. Urgent coronary angiography and laboratory markers excluded acute coronary syndrome. Echocardiography and cardiac MRI revealed a large myocardial metastasis infiltrating the right ventricle and interventricular septum.
